ops.title: This sandbox's uv has an exclude-newer cutoff that blocks just-published package versions; the working override is the UV_EXCLUDE_NEWER env var plus a cache refresh
ops.body:
This environment's `uv`/`uvx` enforces an `exclude-newer` timestamp cutoff (for
reproducibility) from a uv config file, not an env var. A package version published
*after* the cutoff fails to resolve ("only <pkg><=<old> is available").

Verified working override (capt-hook 9.28.0 release day, 2026-07-18):

    UV_EXCLUDE_NEWER=<future ISO e.g. 2026-07-19T00:00:00Z> uvx --isolated --no-cache '<pkg>>=<v>' ...

Three traps measured that day:
- `--exclude-newer-package '<pkg>=<date>'` alone did NOT take (resolution still refused);
  the env var override is what works. `UV_EXCLUDE_NEWER=` (empty) errors — the config-file
  pin cannot be cleared, only overridden with a valid date/duration.
- The simple-index HTTP cache goes stale independently: even with the env override, a
  cached index page hides the new version. One `--no-cache` run does not warm the cache
  (bypasses read AND write); use `--refresh-package <pkg>` once to warm it, after which
  plain runs resolve the new version.
- Env beats config file; CLI beats env (uv precedence).

Relevant when doing the release-then-verify loop for the user's published packages
(capt-hook, cc-context, cc-notes, cc-transcript, spawnllm). Related: [[capt-hook-local-testing]]
(uvx = published wheel; use `uv run` for local source).
